handoff_goal: Plan a globally available internal agent tool capability that lets the agent inspect, explain, validate, create, update, duplicate/copy, and delete workflow templates through conversation while preserving existing Workflows validation, persistence, field guidance, permission, Settings compatibility, and session-snapshot safety rules.

current_project_roles.scope: Built-in agent tools, workflow template registry/API, workflow template persistence, workflow runtime/session behavior, workflow tool policy, and desktop workflow management surfaces.
current_project_roles.evidence_source: User request plus live repository reads.
current_project_roles.notes: The user is extending the current repository's Workflows feature.

target_project_roles.scope: Add an internal tool capability so agents can author and mutate workflow templates from ordinary chat.
target_project_roles.evidence_source: User request plus live repository reads.
target_project_roles.notes: No cross-project transfer or external target was requested.

implementation_target.target_project_cognition_status: fresh/query_ready during plan intake; advisory only
implementation_target.cognition_authority_rule: Project cognition narrows navigation only; implementation facts must be backed by live repository reads and tests.

source_signal_disposition.signal: Provide an internal agent tool capability for conversational workflow template authoring and optimization.
Create workflow from natural-language phases and persist it for Settings refetch.
Optimize an existing workflow through a validated update or safe copy.
First scope includes list, inspect, field guide, validate, create, update, duplicate/copy, and delete.
Valid user-template create/update writes directly without mandatory preview.
Tool is globally available in ordinary chat but respects active workflow phase action policy.
Builtin templates are read-only mutation targets; builtin edits copy to user template.
Update/delete re-read current template and reject stale writes.
Delete is destructive and only valid for uniquely identified user templates.
Builtin copy naming uses <builtin-id>-custom with numeric suffixes and <Builtin Name> Custom.
Agent must have discoverable schema-aligned field guide.
Do not mutate protected non-workflow user state.
Do not support parallel, nested, branching, or loop workflows in first scope.
Do not mutate active or historical workflow session snapshots.
Reuse or share existing registry/API validation and persistence semantics.
Live source evidence, not stale/blocked project cognition, proves current project facts.
Option A direct validated authoring tool is selected.
Invalid, risky, stale, ambiguous, or permission-denied operations must not write and must return actionable diagnostics.
Settings Workflows remains compatible and can display agent-authored templates after reload/refetch.
Tool results must make persistence state auditable in transcript.
Decide whether desktop-launched sessions need a desktop-server-backed path.
Decide how the field guide stays aligned with validation.

must_preserve: Direct validated writes for user-template create/update; no mandatory preview step.
Global ordinary-chat availability with active workflow phase-policy and permission gating.
Builtin templates are read-only and modified only through copy-then-edit.
Delete only uniquely identified user templates and treat delete as destructive.
Update/delete stale-write rejection; create current id conflict checks.
Field guide must be discoverable and validation-aligned.
Settings Workflows compatibility through the same persisted registry/store after reload/refetch.
Template edits affect future workflow sessions only.
No protected non-workflow user state mutation.
